title: Alangium minahassicum
taxonomy: Alangium minahassicum is a species of flowering plant in the family Cornaceae. It is native to Sri Lanka and is part of the genus Alangium, which consists of several species of shrubs and small trees. The plant is further classified as belonging to the order Cornales and the clade Asteridae. This classification places Alangium minahassicum within the broader context of flowering plant evolution. The specific classification of the plant provides a framework for understanding its relationships with other species in the plant kingdom.
uses: Alangium minahassicum is reported to have various medicinal uses, including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. Its properties also suggest potential use in combating infections and promoting wound healing. Additionally, the plant may have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which could aid in the treatment of certain diseases. Research into the plant's medicinal uses is ongoing, and more studies are needed to fully understand its potential benefits. Further investigation into Alangium minahassicum could lead to the development of new treatments for various medical conditions.

intro: Alangium minahassicum, also known as the Minahass Alangium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cornaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can help alleviate various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Alangium minahassicum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects.
